technique: Web shell upload then command execution on an IIS/APP host

trigger.from_breadth: An IIS/APP lead suggesting a web shell: a POST to a script-extension path (.aspx/.asp/.ashx/.php/.jsp/.cfm) returning 200; a known webshell filename in the URI (c99/r57/wso/b374k/cmd.aspx/shell.*/FilesMan); a path that was 404 earlier and is now 200 (newly dropped file); a POST to a static resource (.jpg/.png/.gif) returning 200; or webshell-interaction tokens (cmd=/eval=/exec=/whoami/net user/powershell) on a script URI.

telemetry_required: IIS URI_Stream (path) + cs-method + Status + Client_ip (OP-GL parses these; PROD/AZ/DEV frequently NULL -> hunt the raw W3SVC message)
Server_Bytes / sc_bytes (response size) to size command output
Windows 4688 on the IIS host (w3wp child process) for execution confirmation
telemetry_gap_behavior: No file-integrity monitoring on IIS webroots, and PROD/AZ/DEV null the parsed URI/Status -> hunt the path/method/status in the raw W3SVC message. A parsed-field zero is NOT clean until the raw message is swept. If 4688 is unavailable on the host, execution can only be inferred from IIS response patterns -> note the KNOWN-GAP, never call clean.

method.compute: URI_Stream with a script extension reached by POST returning 200, OR a known webshell filename, OR a path that flipped 404->200 inside the window. Record first-seen timestamp, method, status, and the requesting Client_ip(s).
POST/PUT to the path (or to an upload endpoint) returning 2xx; large cs_bytes (payload body). A script file now returning 200 that previously 404'd = dropped shell.
method.decision_input: successful upload (2xx) of a script-extension file = web shell installed (Persistence)

method.goal: Confirm EXECUTION (the catch that separates a drop from an active shell)
method.compute: subsequent GET/POST to the shell path carrying command params (cmd= / exec= / eval= / whoami / net+user / powershell / dir / type) returning 200; OR a script URI returning 200 with a tiny/odd response size (command output); OR a POST to a static .jpg/.png/.gif returning 200.
method.decision_input: command interaction returning 200 = active, interacted-with web shell -> CRITICAL

method.compute: which Client_ip(s) reached the shell -> enrich via ioc-ip-reputation; what other paths those IPs touched; geo/ASN against the GEO-ACL
method.decision_input: shell reached from a TI-malicious or non-ACL IP raises confidence

method.compute: on the IIS host, did w3wp.exe spawn a child process (4688 with ParentProcessName w3wp.exe -> cmd.exe/powershell.exe)? did the shell account then move?
method.decision_input: w3wp spawning a shell = confirmed code execution on the server -> chain to lateral-movement if the account pivoted

decision.CRITICAL_conf5: shell path returns 200 to command-execution params, OR a 404->200 script file is being interacted with, OR w3wp spawned a shell on the host
decision.HIGH_conf4: successful upload/drop (2xx) of a script-extension file but no command interaction observed yet
decision.MEDIUM_conf3: webshell filename / interaction pattern requested but blocked (403/404) = attempt (still reported)
decision.FP_kill: legitimate app endpoints that accept POST and return 200 (login, file-upload features) with NO command tokens and a normal response size; known dev/test deploys of new pages; Imperva / Site24x7 health UAs

false_positive_checks: legitimate upload/import features of the app (baseline the endpoint; no command tokens, expected response size)
known-good script endpoints in the site's own baseline (a path that is always 200 is not a 404->200 flip)
OP-GL benign baseline: PAL SyncService.svc, UA Imperva (health), UA Site24x7 monitors
allow-listed scanners (Nessus/Tenable) hitting webshell-name paths during a scan

output_findings_json.query_template: <the IIS path/method/status query that fired> ; confirm exec = same path with cmd/whoami/powershell params returning 200
